title: betula platyphylla subsp. mandshurica
intro: Betula platyphylla subsp. mandshurica, also known as Korean or Manchurian birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of Betula platyphylla subsp. mandshurica include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. These benefits include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving cardiovascular health due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. Betula platyphylla subsp. mandshurica is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, and its leaves and bark are also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as infusions and tinctures.
